evals.name: noisy-rule-classification
evals.prompt: Review lint rule health using the fixture config at evals/files/noisy/.bully.yml and the telemetry log at evals/files/noisy/bully-state/log.jsonl. Pass these explicit paths to the analyzer (--config and --log).
evals.expected_output: A punch list that flags the noisy rule and recommends tightening or demoting; no edits to .bully.yml.
expectations: The transcript shows `analyzer.py` was invoked against the fixture log path (evals/files/noisy/bully-state/log.jsonl)
The final response names the noisy rule (the one with violation_rate above the default 0.5 threshold) in a punch list
The recommendation for the noisy rule is to tighten the pattern, narrow the scope, or demote severity
The transcript does NOT show a write/edit to evals/files/noisy/.bully.yml
The final response references handing off to the bully-author skill for application

evals.name: dead-rule-classification
evals.prompt: Review lint rule health using evals/files/dead/.bully.yml and evals/files/dead/bully-state/log.jsonl. Pass these explicit paths to the analyzer (--config and --log).
evals.expected_output: The dead rule is flagged with a remove-or-recheck-scope recommendation.
expectations: The final response classifies the dead rule (zero fires in the log) as dead
The recommendation either suggests removal or recommends checking the scope glob first
The transcript does NOT show a write/edit to evals/files/dead/.bully.yml

evals.name: slow-rule-classification
evals.prompt: Review lint rule health using evals/files/slow/.bully.yml and evals/files/slow/bully-state/log.jsonl. Pass these explicit paths to the analyzer (--config and --log).
evals.expected_output: The slow rule is flagged with a cache/CI/scope recommendation.
expectations: The final response classifies the slow passthrough rule as slow
The recommendation suggests caching, narrowing scope, demoting severity, or moving to pre-commit/CI
The transcript does NOT show a write/edit to evals/files/slow/.bully.yml

evals.name: empty-log-refusal
evals.prompt: Review lint rule health using evals/files/empty/.bully.yml and evals/files/empty/bully-state/log.jsonl. Pass these explicit paths to the analyzer (--config and --log).
evals.expected_output: The skill refuses to classify and tells the user to make edits first.
expectations: The final response does NOT classify any rule as dead, noisy, or slow
The final response tells the user to make some edits first or otherwise indicates the log is empty/insufficient
